# terrible_dellemc_openmanage_idrac_storage_volume (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `capacity` (Number) Virtual disk size in GB.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `controller_id` (String) Fully Qualified Device Descriptor (FQDD) of the storage controller, for example 'RAID.Integrated.1-1'. Controller FQDD is required for `create` RAID configuration.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `disk_cache_policy` (String) Disk Cache Policy.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `job_wait` (Boolean) This parameter provides the option to wait for the job completion. This is applicable when *state* is `create` or `delete`.
- `job_wait_timeout` (Number) This parameter is the maximum wait time of *job_wait* in seconds. This option is applicable when *job_wait* is `true`.
- `media_type` (String) Media type.
- `number_dedicated_hot_spare` (Number) Number of Dedicated Hot Spare.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `protocol` (String) Bus protocol.
- `raid_init_operation` (String) This option represents initialization configuration operation to be performed on the virtual disk.
- `raid_reset_config` (String) This option represents whether a reset config operation needs to be performed on the RAID controller. Reset Config operation deletes all the virtual disks present on the RAID controller.
- `read_cache_policy` (String) Read cache policy.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `span_depth` (Number) Number of spans in the RAID configuration. *span_depth* is required for `create` and its value depends on *volume_type*.
- `span_length` (Number) Number of disks in a span. *span_length* is required for `create` and its value depends on *volume_type*.
- `state` (String) `create`, performs create volume operation. `delete`, performs remove volume operation. `view`, returns storage view.
- `stripe_size` (Number) Stripe size value to be provided in multiples of 64 * 1024.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `time_to_wait` (Number) The maximum wait time before shutdown in seconds for the Server Configuration Profile (SCP) import operation. This option is applicable when *state* is `create` or `delete`.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `volume_id` (String) Fully Qualified Device Descriptor (FQDD) of the virtual disk, for example 'Disk.virtual.0:RAID.Slot.1-1'. This option is used to get the virtual disk information.
- `volume_type` (String) Provide the the required RAID level.
- `volumes` (String) A list of virtual disk specific iDRAC attributes. This is applicable for `create` and `delete` operations. For `create` operation, name and drives are applicable options, other volume options can also be specified. The drives is a required option for `create` operation and accepts either location (list of drive slot) or id (list of drive fqdd). In iDRAC8, there is no pre-validation for the state of drives. The disk ID or slot number of the drive provided may or may not be in Ready state. Enter the disk ID or slot number of the drive that is already in Ready state. For `delete` operation, only name option is applicable. See the examples for more details.
- `write_cache_policy` (String) Write cache policy.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `error_info` (String) Details of the HTTP Error.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `msg` (String) Overall status of the storage configuration operation.
- `storage_status` (String) Storage configuration job and progress details from the iDRAC.
